*   **Fig. 1: Cross-sectional schematic of the can-type combustor (left) and locations of temperature sensors (right).**

    *   Components: Nozzles, Combustion Chamber, Exhaust Duct
    *   Temperature Sensors Location: TC1, TC2, TC3, TC4, with radial locations indicated as multiples of R (0.3R, 0.5R, 0.65R, 0.9R) originating from center.
*   **Fig. 2: Computational domain of the model gas-turbine combustor:**

    *   **(a) Isometric view of nozzle assembly and combustor chamber:** Components: Nozzle, Combustion chamber, Outlet
    *   **(b) Front view of the 5-around-1 configuration:** Components: Center Nozzle (CN), Outer Nozzles (ON1, ON2, ON3, ON4, ON5)
    *   **(c) Cross-section A-A showing the locations of fuel and oxidizer inlets:**
       Components: Outer Nozzle 3,  blank oxidizer inlets, pilot air inlets, pilot fuel inlets, main air inlets, blank passage, pilot passage, main passage
*   **Fig. 2. Computational Domain:** Components: Computational Domain, Temperature Sensors, Nozzles, Combustion Chamber, Exhaust Duct

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   51.6 ppm: Certified NO standard gas concentration (N₂ balance)
*   ±0.64%: Experimental uncertainty range for PSR
*   ±0.5%: Experimental uncertainty range for temperature
*   ±0.64%: Experimental uncertainty range for NO emission
*   ±1.11%: Experimental uncertainty range for NO emission
*   125.65 mm: Radius of outer nozzles(ON1-ON5) from the centerline
*   1.3 mm to 2.5 mm: Range of maximum cell sizes tested in the fine-mesh region.
*   1.5 mm: Cell size for which differences in temperature, axial velocity, and NO emission were less than 7.26% compared to a cell size of 1.3 mm.
*   1.5 mm: Adopted unstructured polyhedral cell maximum size
*   13.6 million: Approximate number of cells generated in the full domain.

title: 1-s2.0-S1743967125003848-main
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text and figures:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   PSR = (m\_FCP / (m\_fuel\_total - m\_FC1)) * 100%
*   FC2%/2 = FC3%/3 = (FC2% + FC3%)/5 = (1 - (FC1% + FCP%))/5

**2. Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Operating conditions and composition of fuel and oxidizer.**
    *   Headers: Parameter [unit], Value, Fuel, Oxidizer
    *   Data:
        *   Operating pressure [bar]: 1.5, -, -
        *   Inlet temperature [K]: 473.15, 718.15
        *   Total mass flow rate [kg/h]: -, 4800
        *   Inlet thermal power [kW]: 2092
        *   Equivalence ratio: 0.539
        *   Mole fraction: CH4 0.5, O2 0.21
        *   Mole fraction: H2 0.5 ,N2 0.79

*   **Table 2: Mass flow rates through fuel circuits in each split ratio case in the fuel-staging configuration.**
    *   Headers:  Case, Pilot Fuel Split ratio [%], Fuel circuit percentages [%], FC1 %, FCP%, FC2%, FC3%
    *   Data
        *   1, 11, 12.00, 09.68, 31.33, 46.99
        *   2, 13, -, 11.44, 30.62, 45.94
        *   3, 15, -, 13.20, 29.92, 44.88
        *   4, 17, -, 14.96, 29.22, 43.82

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 3: Computational meshes:** Isometric View and Cross-section view
    *   Components and labels:
        * nozzle
        * combustion chamber
        * fine mesh

*   **Fig. 4: Schematic diagram of the fuel-staging configuration:**
    *   Components and labels:
        *   FC1: Fuel Circuit 1
        *   FCP: Fuel Circuit Pilot
        *   FC2: Fuel Circuit 2
        *   FC3: Fuel Circuit 3
        *   ON: Outer Nozzle
        *   CN: Center Nozzle

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Fuel: Methane (CH4) and Hydrogen (H2) with 50% mole fraction each
*   Oxidizer: 21% Oxygen (O2) and 79% Nitrogen (N2) by volume
*   FC1 % fixed at 12%
*   PSR changed from 11% to 17% in 2% increments (resulting in PSR11%, PSR13%, PSR15%, PSR17% cases).

title: 1-s2.0-S1743967125003848-main
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here is the extracted technical content from the provided text and image, organized by category:
**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   d(rho)/dt +nabla.(rho*v) = 0
*   d(rho*v)/dt + nabla.(rho*v*v) = -nabla*p + nabla.(tau) + rho*g + F
*   d(rho*E)/dt + nabla.(v*(rho*E + p)) = nabla.(k_eff*nabla*T - sum(J_i*h_i)) + (tau_eff:v) + S_h
*   d(rho*Y_i)/dt + nabla.(rho*v*Y_i) = -nabla.J_i + R_i
*   J_i = -(rho*D_i,m + mu_t/Sc_t) * nabla*Y_i - D_T,i * (nabla*T/T)
*   rho = (p/((R/M_w)*T))

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 5:** Comparison of experimental and numerical gas temperatures for PSR15% at four sensor locations.
    *   **Axes:**
        *   X-axis: Sensor position (TC1, TC2, TC3, TC4)
        *   Y-axis (left): Temperature [°C] (500 to 1400)
        *   Y-axis (right): Deviation [%] (0 to 45%)
    *   **Curves:**
        *   Expt. PSR15%
        *   Num. PSR15%
        *   Deviation
    *   **Key Values:**
        *   Deviation values at each TC location: -3.01% (TC1), 3.97% (TC2), 1.32% (TC3), 9.56% (TC4)

**3. Tables:**

*   No tables found.

**4. Diagrams:**

*   The text refers to Figure 1 (not shown), which illustrates the location of the thermocouples (TC1, TC2, TC3, TC4) at 1510 mm downstream of the nozzle inlets.
*   The temperature sensors are arranged around the inside of the combustor, TC1 to TC4.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   The GRI 3.0 kinetic model used for NO\_x formation process.
*   TC1, TC2, TC3, TC4: Thermocouple locations.
*   x = 32 mm: Location of the connection between nozzles and combustor parts (nozzle exit).
*   x = -373 mm: Nozzle inlet position.
*   x = 1679 mm: Combustor exit position.
*   PSR11%: Percentage of Partially Stirred Reactor (PaSR) investigated in velocity analysis.
*   Measurement repeatability range: +-30K to +-84 K (from TC1 to TC4)
* Maximum deviation between simulation and experiment at TC4: 90.71 K (9.56%)

Here's the technical information extracted from the provided text:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**

    *   U_m= ((f - f_avg)^2)_avg / (f_avg (1 - f_avg))

2.  **Graphs/Charts:**

    *   Figure 10: Particle path lines originating from the center nozzle and the outer nozzle 3 in the case of PSR11%.
        *  Components: Combustor entrances, combustion chamber, Liner exit
        *  Labels along the bottom: 32, 232, 432, 632, 832, 1032, 1232, 1432, 1632

3.  **Tables:** NO-VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams:** NO-VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

    *   Temperatures exceeded 1800 K throughout the combustor.
    *   Tref = 1800 K (reference temperature)
    *   x = 532 mm to 932 mm (downstream location)
    *   PSR increased from 11% to 17%
    *   ON residence time rose from 27.8 ms to 35.6 ms
    *   Average temperature of ON increased from 1626.95 K to 1664.97 K.
    *   Average temperature of CN remained between 1801.94 K and 1807.78 K.
    *   Residence time of CN varied only from 32.71 ms to 33.03 ms.
    *   Swirl number of ON increased from 0.8007 at PSR 11% to 0.8032 at PSR 17%.
    *   Swirl number of CN remained nearly constant, varying little from 0.7082 to 0.7084.
    *   Residence time, T_R (residence time of reactants)
    *   f=1 pure fuel, f=0 pure oxidizer
    *   x = -250 mm
    *   x = 30 mm,
    *   x = -100 mm
    *   x = 500 mm,
    *   x= -20 mm
    *   x= 32 mm,
    *   Unmixedness decreased steadily and reached a small value in the order of 10^-4
    *   Streamlines from the CN exhibited higher average temperature, ranging from 1802 K to 1808 K
    *   Streamlines from the ON, which ranged from 1627 K to 1665 Κ.

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 10]
Here's the extracted technical content from the gas turbine research page:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**

*   NO15%02 = NOmeas * (20.95% - O2basis) / (20.95% - O2max)
*   x = (X-Xmin) / (Xmax - Xmin) + 1
*   Ūm (PSR) = 2.52 * PSR^2 - 8.23 * PSR + 7.71
*   TR(PSR) = 1.04 × PSR - 0.07
*   NŌ=C×Ūm (PSR) ^a×TR(PSR) ^b

2.  **Graphs/Charts:**

    *   **Fig. 13: NO emissions from experimental (blue dashed line) and numerical (blue solid line) results and the maximum unmixedness from numerical simulations (red solid line) as a function of Pilot Split Ratio (PSR).**

        *   **Y-axis (left):** NO Emission @15% O2 [ppm] - linear scale from 4 to 14. Data points at roughly: 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14.
        *   **Y-axis (right):** Unmixedness - linear scale from 0.0010 to 0.0040. Data points at roughly: 0.0010, 0.0015, 0.0020, 0.0025, 0.0030, 0.0035, 0.0040.

    *   **Fig. 14: Relative contribution of normalized unmixedness (red dashed line) and residence time (blue dashed line) to NO emission and normalized NO emission (black solid line); upward and downward arrows indicate the ratios of increment and decrement in NO emission, respectively, when PSR varies.**
